activities_projects.title: Variegated Leaf Starch Test
activities_projects.objective: Determine if chlorophyll is necessary for photosynthesis
materials: Potted plant with variegated leaves (e.g., Coleus or Money plant)
steps: Keep the plant in sunlight for several hours
Pluck a leaf and map the green and non-green areas on paper
De-chlorophyll the leaf by boiling it in alcohol using a water bath
Wash the leaf and treat it with iodine solution
Compare the blue-black areas with your original map
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Concludes that photosynthesis only occurs in the presence of chlorophyll
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of the mapping and correlation with starch presence (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Rate of Transpiration vs. Humidity
activities_projects.objective: Investigate how moisture in the air affects water loss in plants
steps: Cover a branch of Plant A with a plastic bag (dry environment)
Spray water inside another plastic bag and tie it over a branch of Plant B (humid environment)
Leave both in the sun for 2 hours
Compare the quantity and size of water droplets formed in both bags
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands that high humidity decreases the rate of transpiration
activities_projects.evaluation: Correct analysis of the environmental impact on water loss (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Light Intensity and Oxygen Production
activities_projects.objective: Quantify the effect of light brightness on photosynthesis
steps: Set up the Hydrilla in a beaker under an inverted funnel and test tube
Place the lamp 10cm away and count the number of bubbles per minute
Move the lamp 40cm away and count the bubbles again
Compare the bubble count (oxygen release rate) at both distances
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates the rate of photosynthesis to the intensity of available light
activities_projects.evaluation: Accurate data recording and comparative analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Plant Respiration Experiment
activities_projects.objective: Prove that plants release CO2 during respiration
steps: Place moist germinating seeds in the conical flask
Hang a small tube containing lime water inside the flask
Seal the flask with a cork and leave it for 4 hours
Observe the change in the lime water
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Confirms that plants, like animals, undergo respiration and release CO2
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of why germinating seeds were used for the test (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Rhizobium and Root Nodules
activities_projects.objective: Study symbiotic nutrition in leguminous plants
materials: Roots of pea or bean plants
steps: Carefully wash the soil off the roots of a legume
Locate the small swellings called 'root nodules'
Prick a nodule and observe its internal color (often pinkish)
Research the relationship between the plant and the Rhizobium bacteria
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Identifies nitrogen fixation as a symbiotic life process
activities_projects.evaluation: Detailed sketch of root nodules and functional description (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Xylem and Phloem Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Understand the difference between transport of water and food
steps: Place one stalk in red water and another in blue
Wait for 4 hours, then cut cross-sections of the stalks
Identify the colored spots (vascular bundles)
Explain why xylem is primarily colored in this experiment
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Distinguishes the structural location of transport tissues in plants
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of the cross-section observation and tissue identification (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Insectivorous Plant Case Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyze heterotrophic nutrition in specific plants
steps: Research the Venus Flytrap or Pitcher Plant
Identify why these plants need to trap insects despite being green
Diagram the trapping mechanism
Explain the role of digestive enzymes in these plants
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the necessity of nitrogen supplements in certain habitats
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of the mechanism diagram and nutritional reasoning (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Stomatal Density Comparison
activities_projects.objective: Compare the number of stomata on both sides of a leaf
steps: Apply nail polish to a small area on the upper and lower surfaces of a leaf
Once dry, peel off the polish using tape to get an 'impression'
View the impressions under a microscope
Count the stomata in the field of view for both sides
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Discovers that stomatal distribution varies to control water loss
activities_projects.evaluation: Data table showing stomatal counts for both surfaces (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: CO2 Concentration and Growth
activities_projects.objective: Investigate how CO2 levels influence plant health
materials: Two identical small plants in jars
Baking soda and vinegar (to produce CO2)
steps: Jar A: Normal air. Jar B: Add a small container of soda+vinegar to increase CO2
Seal both jars and place in sunlight for 3 days
Observe and record any differences in the greenness or turgidity of the leaves
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes CO2 as a limiting factor in the process of photosynthesis
activities_projects.evaluation: Comparative observation log of the two plant environments (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: The 'Wilting' Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Understand the importance of turgor pressure
steps: Stop watering a plant for 2-3 days until the leaves droop (wilt)
Water the plant thoroughly and observe the time it takes to stand upright again
Explain the role of water in maintaining the plant's physical structure
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects water absorption to structural support and plant health
activities_projects.evaluation: Explanation of the recovery process in terms of cell turgidity (10 marks)
